name: Jimmy Durante
abstract: James Francis Durante ( də-RAN-tee, Italian: [duˈrante]; February 10, 1893 – January 29, 1980) was an American comedian, actor, singer, and pianist. His distinctive gravelly speech, Lower East Side accent, comic language-butchery, jazz-influenced songs, and prominent nose helped make him one of the United States' most familiar and popula…

concerts.label: Benefit: Haym Solomon Home for the Aged and Infirm

works_created.t: Did You Ever Have the Feeling

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Shields
about_quotes.evidence: The transcript mentions 'Jimmy' multiple times, but context ('my brother Jimmy', 'Larry his first clarinet') clearly identifies him as the interviewee's brother, not Jimmy Durante.

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: hear Jimmy Durante doing, “As Time Goes By,” you know it’s, there’s something very touching about it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: "Papa Jack" Laine and Alfred Laine
about_quotes.evidence: I was with Jimmy Durante when I was 19 years old. I was in New York playing with Jimmy Durante.
about_quotes.doc_title: "Papa Jack" Laine and Alfred Laine Interview, 1951-04-21

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Coquille
about_quotes.evidence: He would imitate Jimmy Durante, he would imitate Libby Rache, he would imitate Sam Butera.

about_quotes.speaker: Clyde Kerr Jr.
about_quotes.evidence: I never did meet him but you know, that’s part of my history man.
about_quotes.doc_title: Clyde Kerr Jr. interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rome, New York, August 22, 2006

about_quotes.speaker: Sharkey Bonano
about_quotes.evidence: Yes, we made some records with Jimmy Durante. In fact, I worked with Jimmy Durante, oh I think it was about, I'd say about three or four months in a place called the College Inn on 125th Street.
